title: 3GL Function Names and the 3GL Include File
breadcrumbs: 3GL Function Names and the 3GL Include File
text: 3GL Function Names and the 3GL Include File For new development, you should include the 3GL include file h3gl.h for all platforms and all 3GL purposes, and call all functions in lowercase only. The naming of Uniface 3GL functions has often been a source of confusion. Historically, all or most 3GL functions were stored in the libraries with uppercase names, and your C code had to call them in uppercase (older 3GL manuals tend to list 3GL functions in uppercase only). However, some functions were implemented in lowercase or even in mixed-case. The platform-specific include file ( umsw3gl.h or h3gl.h ) was intended to hide these inconsistencies from the programmer, but this was not always successful. Additionally, when you wanted to use functions from the call-in API, you had to include the file uactdef.h . Now you have to use only one 3GL include file h3gl.h for all platforms and all 3GL purposes. You must always include the most recent version of the 3GL include file h3gl.h . This provides for backward compatibility of C code that still calls functions in lower- or mixed- case, because it has #define directives for the uppercase variants, for example: #define UPUTAMES uputames When doing new development, you should call all functions in lowercase only. Uppercase is still allowed but it is not recommended. All functions (with only few exceptions) are now stored in the libraries in lowercase. So, if you get uppercase unresolved symbols while linking, you know you probably did not include h3gl.h in your source code. It is not mandatory to use h3gl.h in all circumstances. If you have existing C code that uses umsw3gl.h and/or uactdef.h , there is no pressing need to change this, unless you want to comply with this new version of the 3GL reference material. For new development, we recommend using h3gl.h only. Related Links APIs: 3GL Functions Unless otherwise indicated, all 3GL functions are available on all platforms. Was this topic helpful?
